parts.text: Subject reference 1: preserve identity from this reference.
Footwear reference 1: use this as the exact design reference for footwear.
Footwear reference 2: use this as the exact design reference for footwear.
Headwear reference 1: use this as the exact design reference for headwear.
Headwear reference 2: use this as the exact design reference for headwear.
Apply the compiled catalog edit rules to this target image.

CORE RULES
- Strict catalog edit. Follow every active entity instruction exactly and suppress creative reinterpretation.
- Prioritize realism, product clarity, and commercial usefulness over editorial styling or generative novelty.
- Keep all edits physically believable and scoped to the intended entity only.
- Treat the canonical job as the behavioral source of truth and obey active reference authority boundaries.
- Do not invent optional accessories, props, scene drama, logos, or extra styling not explicitly requested.
- Create one realistic premium catalog image with a clean studio infinity background.
- Preserve the garment exactly: keep color, print, silhouette, length, drape, and construction unchanged.
- Keep the same pose class, camera angle, and framing family while making the pose visibly more professional.
- Use soft premium studio lighting and grounded contact shadows.

SUBJECT LOCK RULES
- Preserve the exact same person: facial structure, skin tone, age impression, body proportions, and hair identity.
- Do not beautify, slim, reshape, or reinterpret the face or body.
- Do not restyle hair beyond minor cleanup of flyaways.
- Preserve the same body identity, shoulder width, torso proportions, limb proportions, and overall human read.
- Keep the same pose class, body-to-camera orientation, and camera relationship while allowing only premium catalog cleanup.
- Head posture may be refined for professionalism, but no exaggerated tilt, twist, or editorial expression drift is allowed.

SUBJECT ANATOMY RULES
- Hands must remain anatomically correct with realistic finger count, joint direction, and natural contact behavior.
- Maintain realistic wrists, elbows, neck, jawline continuity, and clavicle transitions.
- Do not create duplicated limbs, broken joints, melted fingers, or anatomy shortcuts.
- Do not beautify, over-retouch skin, or reshape facial features or body volume.

SUBJECT REFERENCE RULES
- Use the supplied subject reference image set as the strict identity lock for all generated outputs.
- When the target image and subject reference differ slightly, prefer the subject reference for identity and the target image for garment and framing.

GARMENT RULES
- Treat the target image as the garment authority and preserve garment identity exactly.
- Do not redesign garment structure, hemline, silhouette family, print logic, seam map, or lower drape.
- Lock garment color family, fit read, wrinkle logic, and construction detail to the target image.
- Maintain garment material read without smoothing away texture, stitching, or fabric structure.
- Keep any existing pattern crisp, aligned, and unchanged in scale and placement.

FOOTWEAR RULES
- Treat the supplied footwear references as a strict visual lock for exact product identity with no reinterpretation, no drift, and no substitution.
- Replace the original footwear completely with the exact referenced product only.
- Lock silhouette, strap layout, heel geometry, sole shape, finish, and color family to the reference with no structure drift.
- Do not improvise missing details, simplify construction, or restyle the product.
- Do not alter garment length or lower drape to reveal more of the footwear.
- Invalid if silhouette, straps, sole, finish, or color drift from the reference.
- Footwear must attach cleanly to the feet with believable perspective, scale, and ankle alignment.
- Preserve realistic toe direction, heel contact, and sole orientation relative to the body pose.
- Maintain clean contact with the ground plane; no floating shoes, broken shadows, or clipped feet.

HEADWEAR RULES
- Treat the supplied head wrap references as a strict visual lock for wrap shape, coverage, placement, and finish with no reinterpretation or drift.
- Interpret this slot as a compact full head wrap only, not a bandana, loose scarf, soft drape, or generic head covering.
- The wrap must fully cover the hair and sit tightly and smoothly on the head surface.
- Keep the wrap controlled, compact, and consistent with the supplied references.
- No loose tails, no hanging ends, no trailing fabric, no visible knot, and no improvised wrap variations.
- No wrapping under the chin and no front drape behavior.
- Keep the face fully visible and the neck mostly open.
- Do not transfer facial features, face shape, skin tone, hairline, or identity from the head wrap reference images.
- Ignore the identity of the person in the head wrap reference images. Use only the wrap shape, coverage, wrapping logic, rear volume, and placement.
- Invalid if trailing ends appear, the wrap becomes loose, coverage drifts, hair becomes exposed beyond the reference logic, or the result behaves like a hijab-style drape, scarf, or bandana.
- Headwear influence must stay local to the head styling zone only.
- Do not change face shape, skin tone, feature spacing, or any identity-defining characteristic.
- Keep hair interaction believable and premium; no impossible attachment, random volume spikes, or identity drift.
- Do not let headwear hide the face or compete with garment readability.

ACCESSORY: SUNGLASSES
- Sunglasses may sit on the eyes or on top of the head, but must remain minimal, premium, and physically believable.
- Do not distort face shape, eye spacing, nose bridge geometry, or hairline when placing eyewear.
- This accessory may refine only its own item. It must not redesign the garment, shift pose class, or change subject identity.

SCENE RULES
- Replace the amateur environment with a clean studio infinity background.
- Use soft premium catalog lighting, grounded contact shadows, and clean edge separation.
- Use a neutral off-white or light warm studio background with no distracting floor seam or clutter.
- Keep the environment framing-safe for catalog cropping and marketplace reuse.
- Preserve believable foot grounding, subtle ambient occlusion, and floor falloff under both feet.
- Keep the background calm, neutral, and subordinate to the garment and active accessories.

OUTPUT PROFILE RULES
- Return one image only.
- Keep the result suitable for e-commerce catalog use, not editorial styling.
- Do not add props, bags, jewelry, or unrelated accessories unless a slot explicitly requests them.
- Maintain a premium vertical catalog composition suitable for apparel listings.
- Keep exposure even and detail-retentive across face, garment, and accessories.
- Frame for a premium 4:5 vertical catalog output with clean full-body readability.
- Keep head-to-toe coverage stable and avoid awkward crop pressure on footwear, headwear, or hemline.
- Preserve detail fidelity consistent with a 2K catalog deliverable.

GLOBAL NEGATIVE RULES
- The result is invalid if garment details drift or the hemline is shortened.
- The result is invalid if the subject identity changes.
- The result is invalid if any active accessory slot fails exact reference matching, including shape, placement, color family, material read, or product identity.
- The result is invalid if studio gear, background artifacts, or floating-foot errors appear.
- Do not add extra people, extra limbs, random props, logos, text, or watermarks.
- Do not redesign the garment, alter silhouette family, shorten hemline, or change print logic.
- Do not create floating items, broken shadows, detached footwear, or impossible ground contact.
- Do not introduce anatomy corruption, duplicated fingers, broken joints, or distorted facial structure.
- Do not leave background artifacts, studio gear, cutout halos, or dirty masking edges.
- Do not shift white balance into unnatural color casts or overprocess the image into plastic retouch.

REFERENCE BINDING
- Target image = garment, pose class, and framing authority.
- Subject refs = identity authority for face, proportions, and hair.
- Footwear refs = locked design authority for footwear only.
- Headwear refs = locked design authority for headwear only.
- Do not transfer identity from accessory reference images.
Subject reference 1: preserve identity from this reference.
Footwear reference 1: use this as the exact design reference for footwear.
Footwear reference 2: use this as the exact design reference for footwear.
Headwear reference 1: use this as the exact design reference for headwear.
